content.text:
Memory stage: NoteReconciler v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate notes should update active note memory.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_note_id": "existing-note-id-or-null",
              "patch": null,
              "links_to_create": [
                {
                  "to_note_id": "existing-note-id",
                  "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
                  "link_weight": 1.0
                }
              ],
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new reusable context.
        - noop: candidate duplicates an existing active note or is lower value.
        - supersede: candidate is a better current version of target_note_id.
        - update: patch target_note_id without inserting a new note.
        - retract: target_note_id should no longer be active because TARGET_SOURCE retracts it.

        Rules:
        - Compare semantic meaning, not wording.
        - Do not keep duplicate notes just because titles differ.
        - Prefer updating or superseding an existing note when the candidate refines the same decision, rationale, plan, or procedure.
        - Insert when the candidate captures a distinct reusable semantic fragment.
        - Use noop for summary spam, weak paraphrases, or information already captured by an existing note.
        - Use links_to_create only for strong semantic relations between notes.
        - target_note_id must be one of Existing active notes below or null.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate notes below.
        - Return one operation per candidate unless TARGET_SOURCE only retracts or updates an existing note.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 9. Главная мысль: update не должен быть append-only .. 10. Разделение ответственности
Document lines: 277-328

## 9. Главная мысль: update не должен быть append-only

На записи у системы четыре обязательные задачи:

1. понять, стоит ли вообще писать память
2. найти похожие или конфликтующие items
3. превратить input в typed candidates
4. применить policy:
   - `insert`
   - `update`
   - `supersede`
   - `retract`
   - `noop`

Если пропустить retrieval/reconciliation, база быстро зарастает:
- дублями
- stale preferences
- conflicting notes
- task explosion
- summary spam

## 10. Разделение ответственности

### 10.1 User-facing runtime
Делает:
- диалог
- tool orchestration
- read-time retrieval
- cheap routing / escalation

Он не должен тащить на себе тяжелый reconciliation и repair.

### 10.2 Memory worker / manager
Делает:
- routing
- retrieval-before-update
- canonicalization
- note/claim extraction
- reconciliation
- consolidation
- repair
- retention

### 10.3 Storage layer
Отвечает за:
- immutable evidence
- typed tables
- transactional updates
- lineage
- indexes
- audit trail

        Candidate notes:
        [0] type=DIRECTION title=Memory updates must not be append-only and require retrieval/reconciliation summary=The architecture defines a mandatory write pipeline: decide whether to write, find similar/conflicting items, convert input into typed candidates, and apply a policy (`insert`, `update`, `supersede`, `retract`, `noop`). Skipping retrieval/reconciliation causes duplicates, stale preferences, conflicting notes, task explosion, and summary spam. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 9. [1] type=DOC_DIGEST title=Responsibility split across runtime, memory worker, and storage layer summary=Section 10 assigns clear boundaries: User-facing runtime handles dialog, tool orchestration, read-time retrieval, and cheap routing/escalation; Memory worker/manager owns routing, retrieval-before-update, canonicalization, extraction, reconciliation, consolidation, repair, and retention; Storage layer provides immutable evidence, typed tables, transactional updates, lineage, indexes, and audit tra...[truncated 3 chars] scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 10. Разделение ответственности' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md entities=entity:<0> keywords=User-facing runtime,Memory worker / manager,Storage layer,separation of responsibilities tags=component_roles,architecture,operating_model evidence=### 10.1 User-facing runtime Делает: - диалог - tool orchestration - read-time retrieval - cheap routing / escalation Он не должен тащить на себе тяжелый reconciliation и repair.

        Existing active notes:
        id=hot-path:note:<0> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Recommended flow routes all write modes through Write-Time Retrieval and Entity Canonicalizer before specialized updaters summary=The recommended architecture routes noop/direct_structured_write/note_write/mixed decisions from Memory Router, and all non-noop writes pass through Write-Time Retrieval then Entity Canonicalizer before Claim Extractor, Task Updater, Profile Updater, and Note Constructor, with reconciliation and background workers around shared storage. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 7. Когда писать `Claim`, а когда `Note`' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=Claim vs Note,predicate policy,write policy tags=memory_architecture,ingest_policy,claim_note_split useCount=0

A record can combine both independently (e.g., `resolved`+`warm`, `superseded`+`warm`). scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 8. responseFormat.description: Reconcile note candidates against active memory notes.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_reconciler

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.
